description.task: Replace anonymous Runnable, Comparator, and custom functional interface implementations with lambda expressions.
instructions: Run the starter code as-is — it already uses lambdas.
In comments, rewrite the Comparator lambda as an equivalent anonymous class implementation.
In comments, explain: what makes Greeting a functional interface?
Submit the running code with both explanation comments.
description.hint: A functional interface has exactly ONE abstract method. The lambda provides the implementation of that single method. @FunctionalInterface is optional but good practice.
description.expectedOutput:
Hello from lambda thread!
Sorted by length: [fig, kiwi, apple, banana]
Good day, Alice.
Hey, Bob!

description.task: Use the Java Streams API to filter, transform, and collect a list of employee salaries.
description.context: The Streams API enables functional-style pipeline operations on collections: filter → map → collect. It improves readability and can be parallelised easily.
instructions: Complete the first pipeline: add .filter(s -> s > 50000) before .collect(...).
Do NOT change the println statements.
description.hint: Stream shortcuts: filter() narrows elements, mapToInt() converts to IntStream which has average(), count() returns the number of elements after filtering.
description.expectedOutput:
High earners: [72000, 90000, 60000, 82000]
Average salary: 58857.14
Count above 50000: 4

description.task: Use the Java Optional class to safely handle a method that may return null, demonstrating isPresent(), orElse(), and map().
description.context: Optional<T> is a container that may or may not hold a value. It forces callers to explicitly handle the 'no value' case, reducing NullPointerExceptions.
instructions: Run the starter code as-is — do not change it.
Verify all four output lines are correct.
In a comment, explain: why is calling .get() on an empty Optional dangerous? What should you use instead?
Submit the code with your explanation comment.
description.hint: Calling .get() on Optional.empty() throws NoSuchElementException. Prefer .orElse(), .orElseGet(), or check with .isPresent() first. In modern Java, use .ifPresent(consumer) for the cleanest approach.
description.expectedOutput:
Found: true
Name: Alice
Name or default: Guest
Bob's name length: 3
